provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of State and the Secretary of Defense, in consultation with relevant Federal agencies, to submit a multi-year strategy regarding United States support for Ukraine against Russian aggression within 45 days of enactment. The strategy must establish specific and achievable objectives, define and prioritize national security interests, include metrics, estimate required resources on a fiscal-year basis, describe how each aspect of assistance helps Ukraine end the conflict, include a classified independent assessment from USEUCOM Commander on defense articles not yet provided, a classified assessment from the Chairman of the Joint Chiefs on risks to U.S. defense capabilities, an executability and production schedule assessment, information on support to Russia from Iran, China, and North Korea, and be updated quarterly through September 30, 2025.

Requires classified CJCS assessment that provision of defense articles does not pose significant risk to US military capabilities
Requires USD(A&S)/CAPE executability assessment and production schedule

Quarterly updates through September 30, 2025

provisions.description: Requires the President, notwithstanding any other provision of law, to enter into an arrangement with the Government of Ukraine within 60 days of enactment relating to the repayment by Ukraine of economic assistance provided under the headings Economic Support Fund and Assistance for Europe Eurasia and Central Asia in Title IV of this division. Repayment terms to be set by the President. The arrangement may not provide for cancellation of indebtedness except as specified. The President may not cancel indebtedness before November 15, 2024. After November 15, 2024, up to 50 percent of total indebtedness may be cancelled subject to congressional review under SEC. 508. After January 1, 2026, remaining indebtedness may be cancelled subject to congressional review.

provisions.description: Establishes a congressional review process for Presidential actions related to the cancellation of Ukraine's indebtedness under SEC. 507. Requires the President to submit a written report to Congress before taking cancellation action. Provides for 10-day review in 2024, 30-day review in subsequent years (60 days if submitted between July 10 and September 7), and fast-track procedures in both the House and Senate for a joint resolution of disapproval. Includes detailed procedural rules for consideration of the resolution including committee reporting deadlines, floor procedures, debate limits, and coordination between chambers.

provisions.description: Amends Section 2606 of the Ukraine Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2022 to expand loan and loan guarantee authority to cover major non-NATO allies and the Indo-Pacific region, increases the direct loan and loan guarantee ceilings from $4,000,000,000 to $8,000,000,000 each, removes the minimum interest rate requirement, adds exception for FFB loan guarantees, and establishes conditions including Secretary of State certification and congressional consultation requirements for loans to non-NATO allies and Indo-Pacific region.
provisions.title: III
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 304. (a) Section 2606 of the Ukraine Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2022 (division N of Public Law 117–103) is amended as follows:
notes: Subsection (a)(1): Amends subsection (a) of Section 2606 — expands eligibility to major non-NATO allies and Indo-Pacific region, increases direct loan authority from $4,000,000,000 to $8,000,000,000, removes minimum interest rate requirement

Subsection (a)(2): Amends subsection (b) of Section 2606 — expands guarantee eligibility similarly, increases guarantee authority from $4,000,000,000 to $8,000,000,000, adds exception for Federal Financing Bank loan guarantees

Subsection (b): Limits funds for major non-NATO allies and Indo-Pacific to FMFP appropriations in this division and prior acts; requires Secretary of State certification 15 days prior to obligation that loan/guarantee is in national security interest, responds to exigent circumstances, addresses a mutually agreed emergency, and recipient has a repayment plan; requires consultation with committees within 60 days of enactment
Subsection (c): Emergency designation for repurposed amounts pursuant to section 251(b)(2)(A)(i) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985

cross_references.target: Section 2606, Ukraine Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2022 (Division N of P.L. 117-103)
